name: Tinagong Dagat
tagline: The Lake That Breathes with the Sea
description: A hidden saltwater lagoon locked away behind a fortress of jagged limestone, rising and falling silently with the ocean tides — completely out of sight from the open coast. Reached only by pump boat from Calatrava port, it offers calm, sheltered swimming in saline water unlike any typical beach.
insiderTip: Ask a local guide to show you the hidden entry point during high tide.

name: Lapus-Lapus Beach
description: Where massive rock formations carve up the horizon above white sand — a masterclass in raw geological beauty, and the ultimate spot to catch a Tablas sunset without the crowds. Cottages are available for rent, and the calm waters make swimming easy for the whole family.
insiderTip: Look closely at the cliff faces to spot nesting migratory birds.

name: Guindawahan Island
description: Also known as "Guinadauahan ni Bendoy Island," this island destination offers white sand, dramatic rock formations, and rich marine life below the surface. A top spot for snorkeling, often included in island hopping packages.

name: Cove Aurelio
description: A charming beach retreat with natural rock formations and available cottages. Known for its paluto service — bring your fresh catch and have it cooked right at the beach. A favorite for a relaxed, local-style day out.

name: Turtle Cove
description: A beach resort set along the scenic coastline of Linao, featuring white sand, natural rock formations, and cottage facilities for day visitors. Known for its relaxed atmosphere and clean surroundings.

name: Ngak-Ngak Beach
description: A local beach resort along the Linao coastline with white sand and rock formations, offering cottages and a tranquil setting for day trips and family visits.

name: Caw-i Falls
description: A scenic waterfall destination in Balogo, reached by a trekking trail through lush tropical vegetation. A refreshing reward for nature lovers and adventure seekers exploring the interior of Calatrava.

name: Oray Falls
description: A beautiful waterfall in Balogo's forested interior, Oray Falls is a rewarding destination for trekkers seeking cooler air and natural surroundings away from the coast.

name: Tago Falls
description: True to its name, this emerald pool doesn't reveal itself easily. A short, lush trek through the jungle canopy rewards those who brave the trail with a plunge into crisp, cold mountain springs — at 7 to 9 meters, the highest waterfall in Calatrava.
insiderTip: Perfect for a midday reset, when the sun hits the canopy just right.

name: Cawa-Cawa Falls
description: Tucked into the forested hills of Pagsangahan, Cawa-Cawa Falls is a serene waterfall destination surrounded by lush greenery — a quiet contrast to the coastal experience that Calatrava is known for.

name: Hidden Falls
description: True to its name, Hidden Falls is a secluded waterfall deep in Pagsangahan — known only to those willing to venture off the beaten path. A rewarding find for explorers and nature photographers.

name: Pangulo Falls
description: The namesake waterfall of Barangay Pangulo, this destination offers a refreshing natural pool and lush surrounding forest. A peaceful trekking destination on the southern edge of Calatrava's interior highlands.
